diff:
diff --git a/retrofit/src/main/java/retrofit2/Utils.java b/retrofit/src/main/java/retrofit2/Utils.java
--- a/retrofit/src/main/java/retrofit2/Utils.java
+++ b/retrofit/src/main/java/retrofit2/Utils.java
@@ imports: added LinkedHashSet and Set
@@ getParameterUpperBound: changed to loop over upper bounds with visited set
@@ resolve: split into public/private with Set<Type> visiting param; added visiting.add checks

exploration_log.aha_moment: The resolve() method already has TypeVariable cycle detection via the while(true) loop + toResolve==typeVariable check, but ParameterizedType and WildcardType branches make recursive calls without any cycle guard. Adding a visiting set to the private overload prevents StackOverflow for these cases.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could also add cycle detection to Class[] and GenericArrayType branches, but those are less likely to cause cycles in practice.

alternatives: Add a depth counter with a maximum depth limit
Wrap recursive calls in try/catch StackOverflowError
key_decisions.decision: Add a LinkedHashSet<Type> visiting parameter to detect cycles by identity
key_decisions.reasoning: Deterministic cycle detection is more correct than a depth limit, and avoids catching Error which is bad practice

search_sequence.action: Search for getParameterUpperBound and resolve in Utils.java
search_sequence.reasoning: Task targets these two methods directly
search_sequence.result: Found getParameterUpperBound at line 334 (simple WildcardType follow) and resolve() at line 215 (complex recursive method with while loop)

search_sequence.reasoning: Need to understand recursion patterns to add cycle detection
search_sequence.result: TypeVariable branch uses while loop with explicit self-reference check; ParameterizedType and WildcardType branches make recursive resolve() calls without cycle detection

queries.query_text: fix StackOverflowError in Utils.getParameterUpperBound and resolve by adding cycle detection with visited set

solve_notes: Added cycle detection to `getParameterUpperBound()` using a `LinkedHashSet` loop over WildcardType upper bounds. For `resolve()`, split into a public entry point and a private overload that carries a `Set<Type> visiting` parameter; ParameterizedType and WildcardType branches call `visiting.add()` before recursing and guard against cycles.
task_text:
The `Utils.getParameterUpperBound()` method in `Utils.java` resolves
the upper bound of wildcard types in parameterized types (e.g.,
`Call<? extends Foo>`), but does not handle recursive type variables
where a type variable's bound references itself. This can cause
`StackOverflowError` in edge cases with complex generic hierarchies.
Add cycle detection to `getParameterUpperBound()` and
`resolve()`.

test_selection.coverage_skip_reason: Auditor could not generate baseline coverage — see auditor report

thrash_preventing_defs.name: resolveTypeVariable
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: called by resolve() for TypeVariable resolution; understanding this shows why TypeVariable cycles are already handled (loop exits when toResolve==typeVariable), preventing the agent from adding redundant TypeVariable cycle detection

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is the two edited methods. The thrash_preventing set adds resolveTypeVariable() so an agent sees that TypeVariable cycles are already handled by the while loop, and can focus cycle detection only on ParameterizedType and WildcardType branches.
